topic: implement tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa with NARROW SCOPE — Tier-A only (stdlib gap or runtime bug) per raw 159 (hexa-lang-upstream-proposal-mandate). Detector targets: starts_with / find_after / realpath / substring-mismatch / exec-wrap-native workaround patterns + paired upstream-signal presence check. Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) explicitly OUT of scope to prevent false-positive flood and hexa-only enforcement bleed. raw 13/raw 99 hexa-only-strict compliant: pure-hexa selftest fixtures, no external harness; audit ledger at state/hexa_upstream_audit/audit.jsonl per raw 77.
raw_8_compliance: PASS — design-only cycle. NO new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml. NO .raw / .own / .roadmap edits. Only this .json witness lands at declared landing path. Implementation of tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa is SPECIFIED here as a downstream cycle (separate landing); this witness is the design spec, not the implementation.
carry_forward_origin: raw 159 (hive/.raw L5001-5045) hexa-lang-upstream-proposal-mandate FORWARD SPEC at 2026-04-28 registration. follow-up L5043: 'implement tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa - workaround pattern detector (starts_with / find_after / realpath / substring-mismatch / exec-wrap-native) + upstream-signal presence check'. follow-up L5045: 'retroactive sweep — raws 144/145/146/147 all qualify'. 본 cycle 은 그 implementation spec 의 NARROW-SCOPE design — 5 workaround pattern × Tier-A only × upstream-signal pairing. Sibling: cross_repo_upstream_lint.hexa (commit-attribution scope) 와 직교 (cross_repo_upstream_lint = git commit msg scope; 본 lint = source-code workaround pattern scope).
scope_boundary: DESIGN ONLY. Inputs: raw 159 spec L5001-5045 (5 detection-pattern enumeration + Tier-A/B partition + 3 falsifier list). Outputs: (a) 5-detection-pattern matrix with regex spec + Tier-A/B classification per pattern + selftest fixture shape; (b) Tier-A/B boundary decision matrix (rejected: union scope, secondary-only scope) ; (c) selftest fixture PASS/FAIL shape design (4 fixtures min). NO tool/*.hexa file created in this cycle, NO state/hexa_upstream_audit/audit.jsonl bootstrapped — those are SEPARATE follow-up cycles with separate witnesses.

bridge_pick=bridge_gw invocation=tool/gw_observatory_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)

intake.topic_summary: raw 159 follow-up L5043: design tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa with NARROW SCOPE — 5 workaround patterns × Tier-A only (stdlib gap or runtime bug) × upstream-signal presence pairing. Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) explicitly OUT of scope to bound false-positive rate. Detector outputs: PASS = workaround paired with upstream-proposal note (convergence/PR/issue id); FAIL = workaround without upstream signal. Audit ledger schema per raw 77 lands at state/hexa_upstream_audit/audit.jsonl. Selftest fixtures (PASS/FAIL pair per pattern) are the deterministic verifier per raw 53.

alpha_absorbed.applied_to_design: 흡수된 motif = 'apply 5 weak-raw falsifier additions per task-c proposals' — '5 weak rules strengthened with concrete falsifiers' 패턴이 본 cycle 의 '5 workaround detection patterns each with concrete selftest PASS/FAIL fixture pair' 와 직접 동형. 본 cycle reframe: 5-pattern matrix 의 각 row 가 (pattern_name, regex, tier-A/B class, selftest_pass_fixture, selftest_fail_fixture, upstream_signal_presence_check) 6-tuple — 흡수된 falsifier-strengthening shape 직계 적용. 또한 'task-c' (강화) 의 'narrow-scope 첫-도입' analog: T1+T2+T3+T4+T5 강화안 처럼 5 pattern 각각 atomic 강화 단위.

beta_qrng.payload_summary: qrng_byte=4 source=urandom (obs_2 reframe — T1 length=1024 queue active)
beta_qrng.applied_to_design: byte 4 (0x04) → mod 3 = 1 → axis_b hot path (Tier-A vs Tier-B boundary decision). 4 mod 5 = 4 → pattern[4] = 'exec-wrap-native' (5 detection-pattern enumeration L5043 의 5번째). 4 mod 4 = 0 → discovery[0] = 'starts_with' as canonical anchor (raw 146/147 paradigmatic). 4 mod 2 = 0 → Tier-A 우선 (NARROW). axis_b boundary decision 은 본 cycle 의 핵심 trade-off — Tier-A only 채택 근거 명시화 hot anchor. KICK_BETA_REAL_ANU 미설정 → urandom-fallback 정직 라벨.

gamma_bridge.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_gw invocation=tool/gw_observatory_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma_bridge.applied_to_design: GW observatory motif (LIGO/Virgo gravitational-wave detector — narrow-band matched-filter against template bank, false-alarm rate 통제) → 본 cycle 'NARROW' scope 와 자연 매핑. GW observatory 는 'broadband noise + narrow-band signal templates' 로 false-alarm 을 1/(several-yr) 수준으로 통제 — 본 lint 도 'broad workaround corpus + 5 narrow Tier-A templates' 로 false-positive 통제. γ 효과: axis_b (Tier-A/B boundary) 결정에서 'matched-filter narrow band 만 enforce' 채택 근거를 GW observatory false-alarm 통제 패러다임으로 cite. 본 cycle OFFLINE-FALLBACK — 실제 GW catalog fetch 후속 위임.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: five-detection-pattern-matrix-complete
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: axis_a.detection_pattern_matrix 가 raw 159 L5043 의 5 enumerated patterns (starts_with, find_after, realpath, substring-mismatch, exec-wrap-native) 를 모두 포함 + 각 row 6-tuple (pattern_name, regex_or_substring, tier_class, paradigmatic_raw_anchor, selftest_pass_fixture, selftest_fail_fixture) non-empty.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: axis_a.detection_pattern_matrix length == 5 + 각 row 6 필드 non-empty + pattern_name set == {starts_with, find_after, realpath, substring-mismatch, exec-wrap-native}
falsifiers_preregistered.mechanism: deterministic JSON length + field-presence + set membership (raw 53)
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: matrix.length == 5 AND all 6 fields non-empty per row AND pattern_name set match

falsifiers_preregistered.name: tier-A-vs-tier-B-boundary-decision-rationale-cited
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: axis_b.tier_boundary_decision 가 (a) chosen_scope == 'Tier-A only' (b) options_considered length >= 3 (Tier-A only / Tier-A+B union / Tier-B only) (c) rejection_rationale per option non-empty (d) GW observatory matched-filter analogy cited per γ envelope (e) raw 159 L5008 'Tier-A = stdlib gap or runtime bug; Tier-B = ad-hoc convention preference (out of scope)' 직접 cite.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: axis_b.tier_boundary_decision.chosen_scope == 'Tier-A only' AND options_considered.length >= 3 AND raw 159 L5008 string contains AND 'matched-filter' or 'GW observatory' string contains in rationale

falsifiers_preregistered.name: selftest-fixture-pass-fail-pair-per-pattern
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: axis_c.selftest_fixture_design 가 5 patterns × (1 PASS + 1 FAIL) = 10 fixtures 모두 명시. 각 fixture: (fixture_id, pattern_name, fixture_kind∈{PASS,FAIL}, source_snippet, expected_lint_verdict, upstream_signal_presence). length<10 또는 누락 = FAIL.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: axis_c.selftest_fixture_design.fixtures length == 10 + 각 fixture 6 필드 non-empty + pattern coverage == 5 distinct patterns × 2 kinds each
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: fixtures.length == 10 AND all 6 fields non-empty AND distinct(pattern_name) == 5 AND distinct(fixture_kind) == 2

falsifiers_preregistered.name: upstream-signal-presence-check-spec
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: axis_a.detection_pattern_matrix 또는 별도 sub-section 에 upstream_signal_presence_check 의 검출 sources 명시: (a) hive/.raw upstream-proposal-note (b) convergence/* convergence note with hexa-lang target (c) git commit msg co-author / slug reference (d) hexa-lang/issues or hexa-lang/pulls reference id. >= 3 sources 명시.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: upstream_signal_presence_check.sources length >= 3 + 'hive/.raw' OR 'convergence/' OR 'commit msg' OR 'hexa-lang/issues' OR 'hexa-lang/pulls' string contains
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: sources.length >= 3 AND >= 3 distinct source kinds present

falsifiers_preregistered.name: K-ge-3-orthogonal-axes-correlation-lt-0_7
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: fan_out has axis_a (5-pattern matrix), axis_b (Tier-A/B boundary decision), axis_c (selftest fixture design) — K=3 충족 + correlation_estimate non-empty + correlation_lt_0_7=true. axis_a 와 axis_b 와 axis_c 가 직교: pattern enumeration ⊥ scope decision ⊥ verifier shape.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: fan_out 3 axes + correlation_estimate string + boolean true
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: K>=3 AND correlation_lt_0_7==true AND correlation_estimate non-empty

falsifiers_preregistered.name: honesty-triad-fields-met
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: honesty_triad has promotion_counter (int>=0) + write_barrier non-empty (mentions 'no .raw/.own/.roadmap edits' AND 'no tool/*.hexa created') + no_fabrication_evidence[] length >= 4 (each: file:line citation grounding a specific design claim).
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: schema field-type + length + write_barrier string contains check
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: 3 keys + counter int + barrier 2-condition + evidence length >= 4

falsifiers_preregistered.name: narrow-scope-false-positive-bound-named
falsifiers_preregistered.criterion: axis_b 또는 별도 sub-section 에 false_positive_bound 명시 — Tier-A only 채택의 정량적 기대값 (예: false-positive rate per 100 hexa files <= 5; baseline narrow detector 의 expected hit rate). raw 159 falsifier 30d post 의 'upstream-proposal-coverage < 0.20' threshold 에 narrow scope 가 어떻게 contribute 하는지 명시.
falsifiers_preregistered.experiment_or_observation: false_positive_bound non-empty + numeric threshold OR ratio mentioned + raw 159 30d threshold cited
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict_check: false_positive_bound non-empty AND raw 159 30d threshold cited

axis_a.rationale: raw 159 L5043 follow-up 의 5 enumerated workaround patterns (starts_with / find_after / realpath / substring-mismatch / exec-wrap-native) 각각에 대해 (pattern_name, detection_regex_or_substring, tier_class, paradigmatic_raw_anchor, selftest_pass_fixture, selftest_fail_fixture) 6-tuple 명시. β index 4 mod 5 = 4 → pattern[4] = 'exec-wrap-native' hot anchor. α 'task-c 5-pattern' motif 직접 흡수.

detection_pattern_matrix.paradigmatic_raw_anchor: raw 146 prefix-length-mismatch-lint + raw 147 prefix-aware-find-mandate (both surfaced via per-cycle workaround→upstream pattern)
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_pass_fixture: fixture S1: source contains starts_with workaround AND adjacent comment '// upstream: hexa-lang/issues/N stdlib starts_with proposal' → lint PASS
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_fail_fixture: fixture U1: source contains starts_with workaround AND no upstream signal in same commit/file → lint FAIL with reason=missing-upstream-signal-for-Tier-A-stdlib-gap
detection_pattern_matrix.false_positive_disclosure: literal starts_with may appear in unrelated user-defined methods (not stdlib gap surrogates); narrowing heuristic = match only when pattern is followed by a string literal and not preceded by 'fn ' (function definition)

detection_pattern_matrix.detection_regex_or_substring: .find_after(" OR substring 'find_after(' OR ad-hoc find_after via index_of+substring chains
detection_pattern_matrix.paradigmatic_raw_anchor: raw 147 prefix-aware-find-mandate (find_after = stdlib should provide; absent → ad-hoc index_of+substring chain proliferates)
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_pass_fixture: fixture S2: source uses index_of+substring as find_after surrogate AND comment '// upstream: hexa-lang RFC find_after' → lint PASS
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_fail_fixture: fixture U2: source uses .find_after(' workaround pattern with no paired upstream-proposal note in file or commit → lint FAIL
detection_pattern_matrix.false_positive_disclosure: user-named .find_after on user-defined types is FP; narrowing heuristic = receiver type must be `string` (resolvable via simple receiver-name regex matching common string idents like `s`, `name`, `path`, `line`)

detection_pattern_matrix.detection_regex_or_substring: exec("readlink -f...") OR exec("realpath ...") OR exec("python3 -c \"import os.path...realpath\"") OR substring 'realpath(' as ad-hoc canonicalization helper
detection_pattern_matrix.paradigmatic_raw_anchor: raw 145 aot-cache-canonical-path (realpath canonicalization absence → AOT cache symlink corruption)
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_pass_fixture: fixture S3: exec("readlink -f X") wrapped helper with comment '// upstream: hexa-lang stdlib canonical-path RFC' → lint PASS
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_fail_fixture: fixture U3: exec("readlink -f X") inline at dispatch site with no upstream signal in same commit → lint FAIL
detection_pattern_matrix.false_positive_disclosure: legitimate readlink usage for non-canonicalization purposes (target inspection only) is FP; narrowing heuristic = pattern must be the OUTPUT of the exec used as a path argument downstream

detection_pattern_matrix.detection_regex_or_substring: .substring(i, i + len(s) - 1) OR off-by-one length arithmetic in .substring calls — pattern: 'len(' followed by '-1' inside .substring args
detection_pattern_matrix.tier_class: Tier-A (parser/stdlib edge — substring-mismatch is the raw 146 prefix-length-mismatch-lint origin)
detection_pattern_matrix.paradigmatic_raw_anchor: raw 146 prefix-length-mismatch-lint (substring length arithmetic off-by-one is recurrent workaround when prefix-aware stdlib helper is missing)
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_pass_fixture: fixture S4: substring(i, i+len(s)-1) workaround with comment '// upstream: hexa-lang stdlib prefix-aware-find issue/N' → lint PASS
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_fail_fixture: fixture U4: substring(i, i+len(s)-1) workaround with no upstream signal → lint FAIL
detection_pattern_matrix.false_positive_disclosure: off-by-one is sometimes intentional (excluding final char on purpose); narrowing heuristic = combined with adjacent .starts_with or .index_of pattern (workaround-cluster signature, not bare arithmetic)

detection_pattern_matrix.detection_regex_or_substring: exec("<cmd>") where <cmd> matches a hexa-lang stdlib API equivalent — heuristic: exec call whose first token is in {'date', 'realpath', 'readlink', 'cat', 'wc', 'sha256sum', 'sha1sum', 'mktemp', 'dirname', 'basename'} AND a hexa-lang stdlib helper exists for the same operation
detection_pattern_matrix.tier_class: Tier-A (stdlib gap surfacing — every exec wrapping a native API equivalent is stdlib enrichment candidate)
detection_pattern_matrix.paradigmatic_raw_anchor: raw 144 exec-cmd-length-guard (exec-wrap pattern is the recurrent workaround surfaced as upstream stdlib request) — β hot anchor (4 mod 5 = 4)
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_pass_fixture: fixture S5: exec("date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") with comment '// upstream: hexa-lang stdlib time.iso_utc()' → lint PASS
detection_pattern_matrix.selftest_fail_fixture: fixture U5: exec("sha256sum X") inline at hot path with no upstream-proposal note → lint FAIL
detection_pattern_matrix.false_positive_disclosure: exec for unique-to-shell behavior (composite pipelines, find -exec) is legitimately not a stdlib gap surrogate; narrowing heuristic = single-binary exec only (no pipes, no multi-binary chains); whitelist for accepted shell-only operations defined in tool source

tier_class_distribution.rationale: ALL 5 patterns Tier-A by construction (NARROW SCOPE). Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) explicitly OUT of scope per axis_b decision.

sources_recognized: hive/.raw upstream-proposal-note line in same raw rule body (e.g. follow-up upstream:hexa-lang/issues/N)
git commit msg containing 'upstream:hexa-lang' tag OR co-author trailer linking PR id
hexa-lang/issues/<n> URL OR hexa-lang/pulls/<n> URL in source comment within 5 lines of workaround
@hexa_lang_upstream(issue=N) annotation or comment marker on the workaround call
upstream_signal_presence_check.passing_signal_evidence_format: lint emits {pattern_name, source_file, line, upstream_signal_kind, upstream_signal_value} per PASS; FAIL omits upstream_signal_* fields

false_positive_bound.expected_rate_per_100_hexa_files: <= 5 (target — narrow heuristic on Tier-A patterns only; informed by raw 159 30d falsifier '<0.20 = retire OR scope reduction (Tier-A only)' implying current narrow scope is the floor)
false_positive_bound.calibration_approach: selftest fixture set establishes TP=5 + TN=5 baseline; production sweep over tool/*.hexa corpus measures unexpected hits and feeds back into narrowing heuristic refinement (deferred to follow-up cycle)
false_positive_bound.raw_159_30d_threshold_cited: raw 159 falsifier 30d post: upstream-proposal-coverage < 0.20 → retire OR scope reduction (Tier-A only). NARROW design meets the scope-reduction half preemptively.

axis_b.rationale: narrow scope choice 의 정당성 — Tier-A only vs Tier-A+B union vs Tier-B only 3 옵션을 고려하고 Tier-A only 채택. β index 4 mod 3 = 1 → axis_b hot path 와 일치. γ GW observatory matched-filter narrow-band false-alarm 통제 paradigm 직계 cite. axis_a (pattern matrix) 와 axis_c (fixture design) 에 대해 직교 — '범위 결정' axis 분리.

options_considered.name: Tier-A only (stdlib gap + runtime bug)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: CHOSEN — false-positive rate bounded by narrow pattern set; aligns with raw 159 L5008 declaration 'Tier-A = stdlib gap or runtime bug; Tier-B = out of scope'. GW observatory matched-filter analog: narrow templates → controlled FAR.
options_considered.trade_off: may miss legitimate Tier-B 'ad-hoc convention' workarounds; mitigated by separate convention-lints (e.g. existing own_lint.hexa, ext_lint.hexa, doc_lint.hexa) that own that scope.

options_considered.name: Tier-A + Tier-B union
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) catches taste-driven differences (e.g. naming conventions, helper organization) that have no language-gap signal. Risk: lint becomes noise generator; raw 159 30d falsifier threshold (<0.20 coverage = retire) likely tripped because numerator is mostly Tier-B noise without paired upstream signal. Also violates axis_b hot path directive (β favors tighter scope).
options_considered.trade_off: broader coverage at cost of false-positive flood and downstream silencing fatigue.

options_considered.name: Tier-B only
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — directly contradicts raw 159 L5008 ('Tier-B = out of scope'). Convention preference does not surface language gaps; no upstream-feedback-loop discipline gained. Out-of-charter.
options_considered.trade_off: n/a — option doesn't satisfy raw 159 charter.

options_considered.name: Tier-A graduated severity (warn for first 3 patterns, block for last 2)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — adds severity heterogeneity without raw 159 mandate. raw 159 L5041 'severity warn' uniform — graduated severity is over-engineering for v1. Defer to a future cycle if production sweep shows differential fix rates across patterns.
options_considered.trade_off: deferred refinement — keeps v1 simple; may revisit at 30d falsifier review.

decision_matrix.A: narrow scope passes scope-reduction half preemptively
decision_matrix.B: 30d coverage likely <0.20 due to Tier-B noise
decision_matrix.D: same as A but more code surface to maintain

tier_boundary_decision.GW_observatory_matched_filter_analogy: LIGO/Virgo narrow-band matched-filter against template bank achieves false-alarm rate ~1/(several yr) by restricting templates to physically-motivated waveforms. Tier-A only is the matched-filter analog: 5 physically-motivated workaround templates (each anchored to a registered raw rule) → controlled false-positive rate. Tier-B union is the 'broadband threshold trigger' — high event rate, low signal/noise.
tier_boundary_decision.raw_159_L5008_citation_verbatim: 'Tier-A = stdlib gap or runtime bug. Tier-B = ad-hoc convention preference (out of scope).' — hive/.raw L5008 raw 159 scope clause

axis_c.rationale: raw 53 deterministic-verifier-manifest 충족 — 5 pattern × (1 PASS + 1 FAIL) = 10 fixture matrix. 각 fixture: (fixture_id, pattern_name, fixture_kind, source_snippet, expected_lint_verdict, upstream_signal_presence). axis_a (pattern enumeration) 와 axis_b (scope decision) 에 직교 — axis_c 는 'verifier shape' axis. raw 13/raw 99 hexa-only-strict 준수: 모든 fixture 는 pure hexa source, 외부 harness 의존 없음.

fixtures.source_snippet: // upstream: hexa-lang/issues/142 stdlib starts_with\nfn _starts_with(s: string, p: string) -> bool { return s.substring(0, len(p)) == p }
fixtures.upstream_signal_presence: comment line above workaround citing hexa-lang/issues/142

fixtures.source_snippet: fn _starts_with(s: string, p: string) -> bool { return s.substring(0, len(p)) == p }
fixtures.upstream_signal_presence: absent — no comment, no commit-msg trailer, no convergence anchor

fixtures.source_snippet: // upstream: hexa-lang RFC find_after\nfn _find_after(s: string, marker: string, start: int) -> int { let i = s.index_of(marker); if i < start { return -1 }; return i + len(marker) }
fixtures.upstream_signal_presence: comment line above workaround citing RFC

fixtures.source_snippet: fn _find_after(s: string, marker: string, start: int) -> int { let i = s.index_of(marker); if i < start { return -1 }; return i + len(marker) }
// upstream: hexa-lang stdlib canonical-path RFC\nfn _canonical(p: string) -> string { return to_string(exec("readlink -f " + _shell_escape(p))).trim() }
// upstream: hexa-lang stdlib prefix-aware-find issue/151\nlet head = s.substring(i, i + len(prefix) - 1)
fixtures.upstream_signal_presence: comment line above workaround citing issue/151

fixtures.source_snippet: // upstream: hexa-lang stdlib time.iso_utc()\nfn _ts() -> string { return to_string(exec("date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")).trim() }
fixtures.upstream_signal_presence: comment line above workaround citing stdlib helper proposal

selftest_fixture_design.selftest_invocation: hexa tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa --selftest → expects '__HEXA_LANG_UPSTREAM_LINT__ PASS fixtures=10 TP=5 TN=5 FP=0 FN=0' sentinel (raw 80 format)
selftest_fail_modes: FP: PASS fixture incorrectly flagged → narrowing heuristic too aggressive → bisect by pattern_name
FN: FAIL fixture missed → detection regex too narrow → widen pattern within Tier-A
schema mismatch: fixture file format drift → re-validate against this design's 6-tuple per fixture

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a ⊥ axis_b ⊥ axis_c — pattern-enumeration vs scope-decision vs verifier-shape are conceptually independent (a different scope decision would not change fixture shape; a different fixture format would not change pattern set). Pearson rank ~0.15-0.25 across the 3 axes' content vectors (estimated qualitatively from non-overlapping field sets: axis_a covers 5×6 pattern-row content, axis_b covers 4-option decision matrix, axis_c covers 10-fixture verifier table; no shared content beyond the 5 pattern_name keys which are coarse coordinates not metric-correlated content).

axes_surfaced.name: narrow-scope-detection-pattern-matrix

options_considered.name: 5 patterns from raw 159 L5043 verbatim
options_considered.rejection_rationale: CHOSEN — directly maps to raw 159 follow-up enumeration; minimal interpretation drift; each pattern paired with paradigmatic raw anchor (raws 144/145/146/147).

options_considered.name: Add 2 more patterns: 'inline-shell-pipeline' and 'env-var-poke'
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — not enumerated in raw 159 L5043; would expand scope without raw mandate. v1 = verbatim 5; v2 candidate via separate raw addition.

options_considered.name: Reduce to 3 patterns (drop substring-mismatch + exec-wrap-native)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — exec-wrap-native is β hot anchor (4 mod 5 = 4); substring-mismatch is raw 146 paradigmatic. Both are load-bearing for retroactive sweep (raws 144-147 backfill per L5045).

options_considered.name: Single union pattern matched on broad workaround marker comment
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — collapses pattern-specific TP/TN evidence into a single binary; loses per-pattern selftest precision; defeats purpose of paradigmatic-raw anchoring.

axes_surfaced.decision: A — 5 patterns verbatim from raw 159 L5043
axes_surfaced.rationale: Charter alignment + minimal interpretation + per-pattern raw anchor preserves selftest precision and retroactive sweep eligibility for raws 144-147.

axes_surfaced.name: tier-A-vs-tier-B-boundary

options_considered.name: Tier-A only (NARROW)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: CHOSEN — raw 159 L5008 verbatim; GW observatory narrow-band matched-filter analog; β-favored path (4 mod 2 = 0 → Tier-A bias).

options_considered.name: Tier-A + Tier-B union
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — false-positive flood; Tier-B noise dilutes upstream-signal denominator; 30d falsifier threshold likely tripped.

options_considered.name: Tier-B only
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — directly contradicts raw 159 L5008 'Tier-B = out of scope'. Out of charter.

options_considered.name: Tier-A with graduated severity (warn vs block per pattern)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — over-engineering for v1; raw 159 L5041 'severity warn' uniform. Defer to 30d review.

axes_surfaced.rationale: raw 159 L5008 verbatim + GW observatory matched-filter false-alarm-control paradigm + 30d falsifier resilience by preempting scope-reduction.

axes_surfaced.name: selftest-fixture-shape

options_considered.name: 5 PASS + 5 FAIL = 10 fixtures, paired per pattern
options_considered.rejection_rationale: CHOSEN — minimal sufficient TP+TN coverage with per-pattern bisectability; raw 53 deterministic verifier requirement met.

options_considered.name: 1 PASS + 1 FAIL global (2 fixtures)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — loses per-pattern bisectability; selftest sentinel cannot localize regression to specific pattern.

options_considered.name: 5 PASS + 5 FAIL + 5 ambiguous = 15 fixtures
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — ambiguous fixtures invite subjective verdicts; raw 53 mandates deterministic verifier. Defer ambiguous-edge handling to silence-annotation cycle.

options_considered.name: 5 PASS + 5 FAIL + 5 cross-pattern (mixed-pattern source)
options_considered.rejection_rationale: REJECTED — cross-pattern fixtures test interaction not pattern; v1 should establish per-pattern baseline. Defer to v2.

axes_surfaced.decision: A — 10 fixtures (5 PASS + 5 FAIL) paired per pattern

obstructions.name: narrow-heuristic-FP-on-non-stdlib-starts_with
obstructions.description: literal 'starts_with' substring may match user-defined methods unrelated to stdlib gap; narrowing heuristic 'receiver type must be string AND not preceded by fn-definition' is approximate, not type-system-grounded.
obstructions.mitigation: selftest fixture S1/U1 covers the canonical case; production sweep edge cases (e.g. user-defined Trie types with starts_with method) get silence-annotation per raw 159 L5023 ('@hexa_lang_upstream(...)' marker convention). Real type-system grounding deferred to hexa-lang's own static analysis maturity.
obstructions.status: documented; not a blocker for v1

obstructions.name: exec-wrap-native-whitelist-bootstrap
obstructions.description: exec-wrap-native pattern requires whitelist of {date, realpath, readlink, sha256sum, ...} stdlib-equivalent commands. v1 whitelist must be hand-curated; risk of missing a stdlib-equivalent command and misclassifying it as legitimate shell-only.
obstructions.mitigation: v1 whitelist defined in tool source as `let _stdlib_equivalents = ["date", "realpath", "readlink", "sha256sum", "sha1sum", "mktemp", "dirname", "basename"]`; new commands added via amendment commit when discovered. silence-annotation override for borderline cases.
obstructions.status: documented; v1 whitelist is the iteration starting point

obstructions.name: upstream-signal-staleness
obstructions.description: comment '// upstream: hexa-lang/issues/N' may reference a CLOSED or STALE issue (false-PASS); raw 159 falsifier '≥1 declared upstream-proposal-PASS commit shows no actual hexa-lang issue/PR/note within 7 days → false-PASS, classifier bump' targets exactly this.
obstructions.mitigation: v1 lint does NOT verify GitHub API for issue liveness (out of scope for narrow design). Falsification at 7d via separate sweep cycle (deferred). Classifier-version bump path (raw 159 L5029 'classifier-version hexa_upstream_lint.v1') reserved for staleness-detection upgrade.
obstructions.status: documented as deliberate v1 omission with falsifier-driven upgrade path
